forecast.name: Overnight
forecast.detailedForecast: Partly cloudy, with a low around 49. Northwest wind 13 to 16 mph, with gusts as high as 23 mph. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.

forecast.name: Thursday
forecast.detailedForecast: Sunny. High near 63, with temperatures falling to around 61 in the afternoon. Northwest wind 10 to 23 mph.

weather_report:
In the calm embrace of the night, Downtown San Francisco basks under a partly cloudy sky, where the low temperature gently hovers around the cool edge of 49 degrees. A notable northwest wind whispers through the city, playful enough to lift loose papers but not fierce, providing a mild reminder of the sea's breath. As dawn approaches, the clouds may thicken just enough to tease a few fleeting raindrops—barely a whisper of moisture in this ever-dry urban landscape.

As the sun wanders into the sky tomorrow, expect an inviting tableau of bright sunshine, bathing everything in warm light that will peak near 63 degrees before retreating to a comfortable 61. The day's air will dance with a northwesterly breeze, invigorating yet benign. While the morning may present some lingering marine layer, particularly near the coastal edges, a crisp clarity will take hold as the day progresses. This palette of weather invites one to dress casually yet warmly; a light jacket or cardigan will suffice against the breeze, layered over a favorite shirt to provide comfort throughout the day. 

Given these conditions, a stroll down one of the quieter streets could yield delightful surprises—perhaps a moment spent sipping coffee at an outdoor café while watching the city wake, or seeking out a lesser-known park for reflection or reading. Just remember, the charm of San Francisco lies not only in the iconic views but in its hidden corners, best explored when the sun blesses the day and the wind carries promise.
